tracked_player:
TrackedPlayer (/Users/michaeljones/Projects/loanarmy/academy-watch-backend/src/models/tracked_player.py):

Core identity fields:
  - id (int, primary key)
  - player_api_id (int, NOT NULL, indexed) — from api-football; uniquely identifies player globally
  - player_name (str, 200 chars)
  - photo_url (str, 500 chars)
  - position (str, 50 chars) — e.g. "Goalkeeper", "Defender", "Midfielder", "Attacker"
  - nationality, birth_date, age

Parent club relationship:
  - team_id (int, FK to teams.id, NOT NULL, indexed) — parent/owning academy club
  - team (relationship) — backref to Team

Current placement:
  - status (str, 20 chars, NOT NULL, default='academy') — values: 'academy', 'on_loan', 'first_team', 'released', 'sold'
  - current_level (str, 20 chars) — 'U18', 'U21', 'U23', 'Reserve', 'Senior'
  - current_club_api_id (int) — API ID of current club (loan destination, first team, or selling club)
  - current_club_name (str, 200 chars) — friendly name
  - current_club_db_id (int, FK to teams.id, nullable) — DB link to current club if in system

Transfer info:
  - sale_fee (str, 100 chars) — e.g. "€50M", "Free"

Data provenance:
  - data_source (str, 30 chars, default='api-football') — 'api-football', 'manual', 'cohort-seed'
  - data_depth (str, 20 chars, default='full_stats') — 'full_stats', 'events_only', 'profile_only'
  - journey_id (int, FK to player_journeys.id, nullable) — rich career history link

Admin controls:
  - pinned_parent (bool, default=false) — prevents re-assignment of team_id by classifier
  - is_active (bool, default=true)
  - notes (text)

Timestamps:
  - created_at, updated_at (datetime UTC)

Unique constraint: (player_api_id, team_id) — one row per player per parent club

Key method: compute_stats() aggregates from FixturePlayerStats (full_stats) or PlayerStatsCache (limited/events_only), returning dict with:
  - appearances (int), goals, assists, minutes_played, saves, yellows, reds
  - stats_coverage ('full', 'limited', or from data_depth)

Usage notes:
  - player_api_id is the primary identifier to match video-detected faces to tracked players
  - Identify untracked/rival players by setting tracked_player_id to NULL and storing player_name as free text
  - team_id is the parent club that owns the player's development rights
stats_models:
FixturePlayerStats (/Users/michaeljones/Projects/loanarmy/academy-watch-backend/src/models/weekly.py, lines 49-119):

Per-match performance snapshot:
  - id (int, primary key)
  - fixture_id (int, FK to fixtures.id, NOT NULL)
  - player_api_id (int, NOT NULL)
  - team_api_id (int, NOT NULL) — current club playing in this match
  - Unique constraint: (fixture_id, player_api_id)

Basic match info:
  - minutes (int, default=0) — minutes played
  - position (str, 10 chars) — tactical position 'G', 'D', 'M', 'F'
  - number (int) — jersey number
  - rating (float) — match rating 0-10
  - captain (bool), substitute (bool)

Goals/assists/GK stats:
  - goals (int), assists (int)
  - goals_conceded (int, for GK), saves (int, for GK)

Discipline:
  - yellows (int), reds (int)

Shooting:
  - shots_total, shots_on (int)

Passing:
  - passes_total, passes_key, passes_accuracy (str, e.g. "68%")

Defense (non-GK):
  - tackles_total, tackles_blocks, tackles_interceptions
  - duels_total, duels_won
  - dribbles_attempts, dribbles_success, dribbles_past

Fouls/penalties:
  - fouls_drawn, fouls_committed
  - penalty_won, penalty_committed, penalty_scored, penalty_missed, penalty_saved

Other:
  - offsides (int)
  - formation (str, 20 chars, e.g. "4-3-3"), grid (str, "2:1"), formation_position (str, "LB")
  - raw_json (text)

Method: to_dict() returns structured nested dict (shots: {total, on}, passes: {total, key, accuracy}, etc.)

PlayerStatsCache (/Users/michaeljones/Projects/loanarmy/academy-watch-backend/src/models/league.py, lines 134-171):

Aggregated seasonal stats for limited-coverage leagues (no FixturePlayerStats):
  - id, player_api_id, team_api_id, season
  - stats_coverage (str, default='limited') — 'limited', 'full', etc.
  - appearances, goals, assists, minutes_played, saves, yellows, reds (int)
  - updated_at (datetime)
  - Unique constraint: (player_api_id, team_api_id, season)

Use case: TrackedPlayer.compute_stats() reads from this if data_depth is 'events_only' or 'profile_only'

WeeklyLoanAppearance (/Users/michaeljones/Projects/loanarmy/academy-watch-backend/src/models/weekly.py, lines 166-182):

Newsletter-specific aggregations per player per weekly fixture window:
  - id, weekly_report_id (FK), player_api_id, fixture_id (FK), team_api_id
  - appeared (bool), minutes, goals, assists, yellows, reds (int)
  - Unique constraint: (weekly_report_id, player_api_id, fixture_id)

Schema shape exposed to frontend (PlayerPage.jsx line 56-99 METRIC_CONFIG):
  Attacker: goals, shots_total, shots_on, assists, dribbles_success, passes_key
  Midfielder: passes_total, passes_key, tackles_total, duels_won, interceptions, goals, assists
  Defender: tackles_total, duels_won, interceptions, blocks, clearances, passes_total
  Goalkeeper: saves, passes_total, rating

Display components (PlayerPage.jsx):
  - Season Summary Cards: appearances, minutes, goals/saves, assists, avg_rating
  - Performance Trends chart (line chart, selectable metrics)
  - Match Ratings & Minutes Played (separate line/bar charts)
  - Match Log table: date, club, opponent, minutes, rating, G/A, key stats
  - Per-Club Breakdown (if multiple clubs): appearances, minutes, goals, assists, saves, goals_conceded

Team (/Users/michaeljones/Projects/loanarmy/academy-watch-backend/src/models/league.py, lines 74-131):

Identity and structure:
  - id (int, primary key) — internal DB ID
  - team_id (int, NOT NULL) — api-football team_id
  - name (str, 100 chars), code (str, 10 chars)
  - country (str, 50 chars)
  - founded (int)
  - national (bool, default=false)

Venue info:

Operational flags:
  - is_active (bool, default=true)
  - newsletters_active (bool, default=false)
  - is_tracked (bool, default=false) — whether we actively track academy players
  - season (int, NOT NULL)
  - Unique constraint: (team_id, season)

Relationships:
  - league (relationship to League)
  - tracked_players (relationship to TrackedPlayer, backref)
  - newsletters (relationship to Newsletter, backref)
  - subscriptions (relationship to UserSubscription, backref)

Method: unique_active_players() returns active TrackedPlayers for this team (parent club)
Method: to_dict() includes league_name, current_loaned_out_count

  - id, email, team_id (FK), preferred_frequency ('weekly', default)

Writer/journalist accounts — NOT team accounts:
  - id, email (unique, nullable), display_name (unique, NOT NULL), display_name_confirmed (bool)
  - can_author_commentary (bool, default=false)
  - is_journalist, is_editor, is_curator (bool flags)
  - bio, profile_image_url, attribution_url, attribution_name
  - email_delivery_preference ('individual' | 'digest')
  - Placeholder fields: managed_by_user_id (self-FK), claimed_at, claim_token, claim_token_expires_at
  - Relationships: commentaries, comments, journalist_subscriptions, subscribed_to
  - Methods: is_placeholder(), is_claimed()

Note: No team ownership/admin model; teams are seeded from API, managed via is_tracked flag and TeamTrackingRequest
proposed_linkage:
New models for video-match workflow:

VideoMatch (top-level match upload):
  - id (int, primary key)
  - team_id (int, FK to teams.id, NOT NULL) — uploading/owning team
  - match_date (date or datetime)
  - home_team_name (str) — may not be in DB (rival team)
  - away_team_name (str)
  - home_goals, away_goals (int, nullable — may be incomplete)
  - video_url or storage_path (str, 500 chars) — cloud blob reference
  - created_at, updated_at

VideoTrack (detected player instance in video):
  - id (int, primary key)
  - video_match_id (int, FK to video_matches.id, NOT NULL)
  - tracked_player_id (int, FK to tracked_players.id, nullable) — tagged by uploader
  - player_name (str, 100 chars) — fallback name for untracked/rival players
  - team_affiliation (str, 50 chars, nullable) — 'home' or 'away' to identify team
  - shirt_number (int, nullable) — from vision detection
  - position_detected (str, 20 chars, nullable) — inferred by CV
  - frame_entry, frame_exit (int, nullable) — video frame ranges
  - appearance_confidence (float, 0-1) — CV confidence score
  - manual_notes (text) — uploader's tagging notes
  - created_at, updated_at

Unique constraint: (video_match_id, tracked_player_id) OR (video_match_id, player_name, shirt_number) to handle duplicates

VideoPlayerMatchStats (per-player aggregation for the video match):
  - id (int, primary key)
  - video_match_id (int, FK to video_matches.id, NOT NULL)
  - video_track_id (int, FK to video_tracks.id, nullable) — link back to detected instance
  - tracked_player_id (int, FK to tracked_players.id, nullable) — may be NULL for rivals
  - player_name (str, 100 chars) — redundant for display but searchable
  - team_affiliation (str, 50 chars) — 'home' or 'away'
  - shirt_number (int, nullable)
  
  Game stats (from manual entry or CV annotation):
  - minutes_played (int)
  - goals, assists (int)
  - shots_on, shots_total (int)
  - passes_key, passes_completed (int)
  - tackles, dribbles, fouls_committed (int)
  - yellow_cards, red_cards (int)
  
  Meta:
  - stats_source (str) — 'manual', 'cv_annotated', 'api_enriched'
  - is_own_team (bool) — true if player is from uploading team
  - is_rival (bool) — true if opponent player
  - confidence_score (float) — combined CV + validation score
  - created_at, updated_at

Migration conventions (from aw10_*, aw11_*):

1. Use _migration_helpers.py (column_exists, table_exists, index_exists, add_column_safe, create_index_safe) for idempotence
2. Guard all additions with existence checks since production has out-of-band schema changes
3. Use sa.ForeignKey() for FK constraints, op.create_foreign_key() for FK definitions
4. Include UniqueConstraint() inline or via __table_args__
5. Use server_default="false" / server_default="0" for NOT NULL columns to avoid backfill issues
6. Backfill via text() queries with conn.execute() for complex joins
7. Downgrade should drop constraints before columns, drop indexes before tables
8. Order by updated_at DESC for tie-breaking; LIMIT 1 for most-recent singleton

Sample migration approach for new video tables:

  def upgrade():
    # Create video_matches
    op.create_table(
      "video_matches",
      sa.Column("id", sa.Integer(), nullable=False),
      sa.Column("team_id", sa.Integer(), nullable=False),
      sa.ForeignKeyConstraint(["team_id"], ["teams.id"]),
      sa.Column("match_date", sa.Date(), nullable=True),
      sa.Column("home_team_name", sa.String(100), nullable=False),
      sa.Column("away_team_name", sa.String(100), nullable=False),
      sa.Column("home_goals", sa.Integer()),
      sa.Column("away_goals", sa.Integer()),
      sa.Column("video_url", sa.String(500)),
      sa.Column("created_at", sa.DateTime()),
      sa.Column("updated_at", sa.DateTime()),
      sa.PrimaryKeyConstraint("id"),
    )
    op.create_index("ix_video_matches_team_date", "video_matches", ["team_id", "match_date"])
    
    # Create video_tracks
    op.create_table(
      "video_tracks",
      sa.Column("id", sa.Integer(), nullable=False),
      sa.Column("video_match_id", sa.Integer(), nullable=False),
      sa.Column("tracked_player_id", sa.Integer(), nullable=True),
      sa.ForeignKeyConstraint(["video_match_id"], ["video_matches.id"]),
      sa.ForeignKeyConstraint(["tracked_player_id"], ["tracked_players.id"]),
      ... [rest of columns and constraints]
    )
    
    # Create video_player_match_stats
    op.create_table(
      "video_player_match_stats",
      ... [columns]
    )
    op.create_index("ix_stats_video_match_player", "video_player_match_stats", ["video_match_id", "tracked_player_id"])

FK semantics:
  - team_id (NOT NULL): uploader must own the VideoMatch
  - tracked_player_id (nullable): player may be untracked/rival; use player_name + team_affiliation instead
  - current_club_db_id on TrackedPlayer (nullable): allows tracking loan players without knowing destination team
  - Use ON DELETE CASCADE for video_tracks -> video_matches to auto-cleanup
